title: Public Toilets in Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **Berlin's public toilet network** is called Berliner Toiletten and is operated by Wall GmbH for the State of Berlin.
A standard **Berliner Toilette costs 50 cents** per use, payable by coin, card, contactless, or the official app.
**107 of the 278 automatic cabins are becoming permanently free and gender-neutral**; the other 171 stay at 50 cents.
**Train station toilets** (Hauptbahnhof, Friedrichstrasse, Zoo, Alexanderplatz) usually cost around 1 euro and are reliable.
**Department stores and malls** (KaDeWe, Galeria, Mall of Berlin, Alexa) are often the easiest comfortable option in the center.
**Install the Berliner Toilette app** before your sightseeing day, and carry a 50 cent and a 1 euro coin.
